events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess residential garage subpanel at rough-complete. Observations: feeder lands on separate neutral and equipment grounding bars; green bonding screw absent and stored in parts bag. Every breaker terminal holds single conductor. All knockout openings filled. Panel schedule affixed. Working clearance unobstructed.

Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Need apply NEC 408 and 250.32. Key issue: subpanel in detached garage? Residential garage subpanel. Feeder lands on separate neutral and equipment grounding bars; bonding screw absent. In a subpanel (downstream from main), neutral and equipment ground must be separated; bonding screw should not be installed (or removed) so neutral bar isolated from enclosure/ground. That's correct. But NEC 250.32: grounding at separate building/structure. For a detached garage, if feeder supplies panel, grounding electrode required at detached structure, and neutral not bonded to ground at subpanel. If attached garage, maybe subpanel fed from main, separate neutral/ground bars correct, bonding screw absent correct. The scenario says residential garage subpanel. Could be attached or detached. Need determine if pass. Working clearance OK. Single conductor per terminal OK. Knockouts filled OK. Panel schedule OK. Bonding screw absent and stored is correct for subpanel. But is there a code violation? The bonding screw absent and stored in parts bag is fine. However, some inspectors require bonding screw not installed; storing in bag inside cabinet is okay but could be considered not installed? It's a spare part. Not a violation.

Potential issue: If garage is detached, NEC 250.32 requires a grounding electrode system at the detached building. The scenario doesn't mention grounding electrode conductor connected to grounding bar. At rough-complete, maybe not visible? It says feeder lands on separate neutral and equipment grounding bars; all knockout openings filled. No mention of ground rod/grounding electrode. Could be fail if no electrode at detached structure. But we don't know if detached or attached. The term "garage subpanel" could be attached. If attached, no separate electrode required (unless required by local). NEC 250.32(A) requires grounding electrode at separate structure. If attached, same building, no electrode. Since not specified, maybe needs_more_info? But the task asks decide. We can note condition: if detached, verify grounding electrode. But decision maybe pass with condition? The schema has actions/findings. The instructions: If work dangerous or non-compliant, decision must fail. We need be careful.

Let's parse scenario: "A residential garage subpanel is shown with the deadfront removed at rough inspection. The feeder lands on separate neutral and equipment grounding bars; the green bonding screw is absent and stored in the parts bag taped inside the cabinet. Every breaker terminal holds a single conductor. All knockout openings are filled. A typed panel schedule is affixed to the door and working clearance in front of the panel is unobstructed." This seems designed to test subpanel neutral-ground separation. The bonding screw absent is correct. All other items good. So likely pass. But maybe the bonding screw should be installed? No, for subpanel it should not. In main panel, bonding screw connects neutral bar to enclosure. In subpanel, neutral isolated, ground bar bonded to enclosure. The green bonding screw is for neutral bar; absent means neutral not bonded to enclosure, correct. So pass.

Need cite specific observations. Use references NEC 408.3? Actually NEC 408.3(B) maybe? Let's recall: NEC 408.3 Support and Arrangement of Busbars. 408.3(B) Grounding of Panelboards. Panelboard cabinets and one busbar shall be grounded. Neutral not to be grounded? Let's check. NEC 408.3(B): Grounding of Panelboards. Panelboard cabinets and one busbar shall be grounded. The code says panelboard cabinets and one busbar shall be grounded. Exception? For panelb…
